meta.source: Cowork · FRED + TradingView Liquidity Monitor - Jae

credit.label: Improving
decomp: No stress spreading despite the semis rout

vol.label: Contained headline vol, sharp sector break
decomp: MOVE flat at 71.96 — no new bond-vol signal

bias: The session reversed hard: after this morning's broad semis rally, the enabling-layer chain cratered into the close — EWY -7.49%, MU -9.16%, DRAM -10.05%, GLW -13.86%, LITE -7.06%, AMKR -6.29%, SOXX -5.96% vs SPY roughly flat. This looks idiosyncratic to Korea/semis rather than a macro risk-off: VIX actually fell, MOVE didn't move, and financials (ALLY, CACC, COF) all rallied while HY OAS tightened further. Korea gate regressed to 1/3 (EWY back below its SMA20) — stay out. Don't chase the semis dip until the catalyst behind today's reversal is identified; check ASX/APH/POWI/DRAM position P&L given the size of the move. RRP normalizing back to ~$1B confirms the Q2 quarter-end spike was transient, a mild liquidity positive.

counterargs.thesis: Semis reversal is idiosyncratic, not macro
supporting: VIX -0.79% — broad equity vol actually eased
MOVE unchanged at 71.96 — no fresh bond-vol signal
against: EWY -7.49%, the sharpest single-session move in the dataset
MU -9.16%, DRAM -10.05%, GLW -13.86% — the whole chain broke together
Korea gate regressed from 2/3 to 1/3 in one session
USDKRW still pinned right at the 1,550 BOK line (1,550.17)
counterargs.read: A synchronized double-digit drawdown across DRAM/MU/GLW/LITE/AMKR/EWY on a day when VIX fell and credit tightened is a strong signal this is sector- or region-specific (Korea/semiconductor news, guidance, or a single large holder unwinding) rather than a liquidity or rate event. Worth checking for a specific catalyst before treating this as thesis-broken — but until identified, treat today's reversal as a hard stop on adding, not a dip to buy.

trade_table.rationale: Reversed from +4.3% (SOXX) this morning to -5.96% into the close. MU -9.16%, DRAM -10.05%, GLW -13.86%. Identify the catalyst before re-adding.
10Y eased off the cycle high to 4.477% (from 4.497%) and TLT is only -0.94%. Less acute than this morning but still not a tailwind.
Gate regressed to 1/3 — EWY -7.49% and back below its 20-day SMA. USDKRW still pinned at 1,550.17. Confirms staying out was right.
ALLY +0.50%, CACC +4.02%, COF +2.23% all rallied while HY OAS tightened another 5bps to 2.75%. Genuine divergence from the semis carnage.
DXY +0.21% today, still firm though decelerating from +0.32% this morning. USDJPY/USDKRW remain pinned at their thresholds.
HYG -0.41% but OAS tightened to 2.75% — credit itself is not confirming the semis-equity stress. Watch for convergence.
Jul–Sep reserve drain still ahead. Today's sector-specific volatility is a reminder of how exposed levered small caps are to single-name shocks.

catalysts.note: TGA + Reserves print, first new read since Jun 24. Will show if drain rate is accelerating.
Strong print = Warsh hike back on table. Weak print = duration relief.
Bid-to-cover and tail will show whether private demand absorbs supply at these higher yields.
PCE peak narrative confirmed or reversed. Hot CPI = duration selloff + Warsh hike.
APH $165/$170, DRAM $69/$74, EOSE $7.50/$10 spreads all expire — today's reversal changes the P&L picture materially.
Quarterly Refunding Announcement. Auction sizes + TGA guidance for Q3.

plumbing.rrp_delta: normalized from $26.9B — Q2 window dressing unwound as expected

chain.note: Sharpest single-day drop in the chain. $69/$74 Jul 17 spread (2 contracts) now well out of the money. Reassess.
Gave back this morning's HBM-thesis gains and more. No specific catalyst confirmed yet — investigate.
Retraced part of this morning's +10.69% surge. Off $274 ATH. No active position.
Biggest single-session move in the dataset. 14 shares @ $189.66 — still above cost basis but the reversal from yesterday's +15.7% spike is dramatic. Investigate catalyst urgently.
Off $964 ATH, extending the pullback. No active position.
50 shares @ $38.57 — still above cost basis despite today's pullback.
Off $96 ATH. Watchlist re-entry candidate, no active position.
$165/$170 Jul 17 spread still in the money despite today's pullback.
25 shares @ $84.21 — back below cost basis after this morning's recovery.
$7.50/$10 Jul 17 spread (10 contracts). Thesis broken, continuing to slide. CLOSE PRIORITY.

changed_today.label: VIX
changed_today.delta: ↓ −0.79% — broad vol calm despite chain rout

vix.note: VIX falling while semis/Korea sold off hard = idiosyncratic sector event, not broad macro fear. Important distinction.

oil.note: Oil stable. Strait of Hormuz partial reopening narrative holding. No new inflation impulse from energy today.

gold.note: Gold mildly lower. Real yield 2.17% remains a structural headwind. No safe-haven bid despite semis selloff — confirms idiosyncratic read.

move.note: MOVE flat while equities sold off. Bond market not pricing macro stress. Consistent with semis/Korea move being sector-specific.
